summary: Iter 39 — 4th retest of CapitalDetailModal bulk-delete modal-stays-open bug. Main agent removed explicit blur() and setTimeout(setSelectionMode) per iter 38 SECONDARY recommendation, but DID NOT apply the PRIMARY recommendation (add onFocusOutside/onInteractOutside preventDefault to DialogContent). Result: modal STILL CLOSES after bulk delete completes. Same end-state as iters 36/37/38. Primary no-reload bug stays fixed (URL unchanged, 0 framenavigated). NEW RCA: even without explicit blur(), the proximate trigger is now the disabled-attribute-induced auto-blur — when setBulkDeleting(true) fires synchronously after the click, the focused bulk-delete-btn gains disabled={bulkDeleting} → browsers auto-blur disabled focused elements → focus → <body> → outside DialogContent → Radix FocusScope closes dialog.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal still closes after bulk delete. Root trigger now: bulk-delete-btn has disabled={selectedIds.size===0 || bulkDeleting}. setBulkDeleting(true) on line 75 disables the currently-focused button → browsers auto-blur disabled focused elements → focus moves to <body> → outside Radix DialogContent → FocusScope dispatches onOpenChange(false) → onClose() runs → modal unmounts. Removing explicit blur() (per iter 38 recommendation) was not enough because the disabled-attribute itself causes the same focus loss.

passed_tests: Bulk delete: URL unchanged, 0 framenavigated → NO PAGE RELOAD (4th iter PASS)
Backend bulk delete API works (movements actually deleted server-side per optimistic flow)
failed_tests: Modal-stays-open after bulk delete: 4TH STRAIGHT FAIL — total-capital-amount becomes invisible, dashboard reappears behind. Screenshot /app/.screenshots/iter39_after_bulk.png shows full dashboard, no modal.
action_items: MUST APPLY (was recommended in iter 38, NOT applied): Add onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} and onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} to <DialogContent ...> in /app/frontend/src/components/modals/CapitalDetailModal.jsx line 143. This is the standard Radix pattern to prevent FocusScope from auto-closing on focus loss. Even with disabled-attribute auto-blur OR explicit blur, modal will stay open because we explicitly preventDefault on focus-outside events.

ALTERNATIVE/COMPLEMENT (less robust on its own): change bulk-delete-btn so it does NOT add disabled={bulkDeleting}; instead, guard inside handleBulkDelete (return early if already running) and just visually swap text/icon to 'Siliniyor...'. This keeps the button enabled and focused, so the browser does not auto-blur. Combined with removing explicit blur() (already done), this could also work — but the DialogContent guard is the cleaner standard fix.
Recommend BOTH (defense-in-depth): apply DialogContent props AND keep button non-disabled during bulkDeleting.
critical_code_review_comments: Iter 38 PRIMARY recommendation (DialogContent onFocusOutside/onInteractOutside preventDefault) was not implemented by the main agent — only SECONDARY (remove blur) was. The PRIMARY is still needed because disabled-attribute on focused button also triggers auto-blur.
Triple state refresh (reload + refreshCapital + fetchData) at lines 94-96 is still redundant.
Comment at line 90-91 claims 'bulk-delete-btn DOM'da ve focus'lu kalır' but this assumption is false: the disabled attribute strips focus. The comment should be revisited after fixing.
Consider: a single robust solution is the DialogContent guard — it makes the modal 'controlled-only' so it ONLY closes when the parent calls onClose(), regardless of any internal focus shuffling.

success_rate.frontend: 55% (no improvement vs iter 38) — primary no-reload PASS for 4th iter; modal-stays-open FAIL for 4th iter

seed_data_creation: User had 6 cash movements at start. After this run, all 6 deleted (cash dropped to ₺145.000 area; total-capital includes vehicle inventory ₺1.100.000 + cash ₺145.000). Re-seed needed if next agent wants more rows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After 4 iterations the modal-stays-open bug is unresolved. The CORRECT fix is in iter 38's PRIMARY action_item: add onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} and onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} props to <DialogContent> (line 143 of /app/frontend/src/components/modals/CapitalDetailModal.jsx). This blocks Radix Dialog's FocusScope from auto-closing on focus loss, regardless of what blurs the button (explicit blur, disabled-attribute, click outside, etc). This is the production-grade Radix workaround. After applying, run the same flow: login → capital-amount-display → enter-selection-mode-btn → toggle-select-all-btn → bulk-delete-btn → wait 3s → assert is_visible('[data-testid=\"total-capital-amount\"]') is True. Note: cash movements may have run out — re-seed via Kasa İşlemi if needed.
rca_of_the_issue: Even after removing explicit blur() and setTimeout (iter 38 recommendation), the modal still closes because the disabled-attribute on the focused bulk-delete-btn (added when setBulkDeleting(true) runs) causes browsers to auto-blur it. Focus moves to <body>, which is outside Radix DialogContent. Radix's FocusScope detects focus has left the dialog and dispatches onOpenChange(false), which propagates to onClose() and unmounts the modal. The robust fix (still not applied after 4 iters) is to add onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} and onInteractOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} to DialogContent, so Radix can no longer auto-close on focus loss. Modal would then close ONLY via explicit onClose() calls (Kapat / X buttons / programmatic).
